directions.name: Micro-retune the single-level B skew in the peeled 64x384 hot kernel
directions.hypothesis: 8346b48 is the new best base because the pairwise peeled hot loop raised tensor active to 35.06 without increasing register pressure beyond 128/thread or breaking the 2-block register occupancy limit. The next visible limiter is no longer synchronization but shared-memory / LSU feed pressure: hot-kernel mio_throttle climbed to 35.58, l1tex data-bank reads rose to 12.15, and lsuin_requests reached 83.68 while active warps stayed flat at 33.07. The coherent next move is a tiny single-level mapping retune inside the existing B shared layout, such as adjusting the warp-group skew or logical-to-shared column mapping, while explicitly avoiding the previously regressive two-level repack and named-barrier handoff families.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Shared-memory B-fragment load pressure and bank behavior in the 64x384 hot kernel, expressed as high mio_throttle and LSU issue pressure rather than low occupancy.
directions.risk: Feed-path rewrites have a bad track record in this search. This must stay at the level of one skew or mapping tweak inside the current single-stage layout; if it turns into a broader B repack or new handoff scheme, it is repeating disproven ground.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if mio or shared-bank metrics improve only cosmetically without a runtime win, or if the change requires a second staging level or any new named-barrier/subgroup machinery.

directions.name: Specialize Tile384 cp.async producer assignment in the peeled hot path
directions.hypothesis: The hot kernel is now clearly the performance center and its producer side still executes a large amount of looped cp.async issue work for the 64x384 B tile: each steady-state recycle walks the generic stage_a_shared_tile_async and stage_b_shared_tile_async loops, recomputes row and column ownership, and then immediately returns to a kernel whose LSU pipe is already at 83.68% and mio_throttle at 35.58. A bounded follow-up is to keep the same CTA-wide barrier model and same shared layout, but add a Tile384-specific producer schedule so thread ownership and copy counts are straight-line or more tightly unrolled in the peeled kernel.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Producer-side cp.async issue overhead and LSU address-generation work in the 64x384 hot loop, not a synchronization problem.
directions.risk: The upside depends on keeping global-to-shared coalescing intact. A bad thread-to-copy partition can reduce cp.async efficiency or add branches that erase the gain.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the specialization does not lower LSU or mio pressure on the hot kernel, or if it needs more synchronization state than the current restored barrier recycle model.

directions.name: Add a fixed-K peeled 64x96 tail kernel
directions.hypothesis: The remaining 64x96 tail still runs the generic tensor-core loop even though the benchmark shape is fully fixed. That tail kernel is only about 0.9 ms of GPU time, but it still carries roughly 19.56% barrier stall and 15.41% short scoreboard with no visible improvement from the hot-kernel win. Reusing the fixed-K peeled structure and quad epilogue on the last 96 columns is a safe cleanup path that complements the current winner without reopening the failed named-barrier path.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual generic-loop and scoreboard overhead in the 64x96 tail kernel; total upside is capped by the tail's small share of wall time.
directions.risk: This is low risk but also lower ceiling. Even a clear tail-kernel improvement may only move total runtime by a few tenths of a millisecond.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the specialized tail does not materially reduce tail-kernel duration or if the end-to-end runtime change stays within measurement noise.

notes: Diagnosed accepted-best run 20260419_104631_bf16_gemm_v1_8346b48 at 34.655231 ms. Pairwise peeled hot-loop unrolling was a real win: hot-kernel duration dropped from the 2872f92 base while registers/thread stayed at 128 and occupancy_limit_registers stayed at 2. The remaining dominant symptom is hot-kernel shared/LSU feed pressure, especially mio_throttle at 35.58. The previous named-barrier/subgroup handoff path remains strong negative evidence because it regressed badly to 41.745407 ms by inflating the hot kernel to 167 registers/thread and cutting active warps in half, so none of the ranked directions continue that family.
